HDFC Small Cap Fund is a small-cap mutual fund . As per SEBI’s classification, small-cap funds are required to invest at least 65% of their assets in small-cap companies, those ranked 251st and below by market capitalization. The fund uses the BSE 250 Small Cap TRI as its benchmark, which helps measure its performance against a broad universe of similar small-cap stocks . The fund has a strong presence in sectors like Healthcare, Technology, Financials, Automobiles, and Infrastructure. India offers a wide and fast-growing base of small-cap companies, which the fund house believes can deliver

TRI as its benchmark, which helps measure its performance against a broad universe of similar small-cap stocks . The fund has a strong presence in sectors like Healthcare, Technology, Financials, Automobiles, and Infrastructure. India offers a wide and fast-growing base of small-cap companies, which the fund house believes can deliver strong long-term returns. HDFC Small Cap Mutual Fund focuses on selecting fundamentally sound businesses with reasonable valuations, aiming to generate alpha while managing risk effectively. HDFC Flexi Cap is a flexi-cap mutual fund launched on 1st January 1995. These funds follow a diversified equity investment strategy where they invest in companies across all market captialization and adjust allocations based on market conditions. The fund uses Nifty 500 TRI as its benchmark. Nifty 500 represents the broader market and helps the fund compare its returns with those of other companies HDFC Mutual Fund follows a bottom-up approach , which involves paying attention to individual businesses instead of macroeconomic factors. They also pay close attention to

uses Nifty 500 TRI as its benchmark. Nifty 500 represents the broader market and helps the fund compare its returns with those of other companies HDFC Mutual Fund follows a bottom-up approach , which involves paying attention to individual businesses instead of macroeconomic factors. They also pay close attention to Quantitative (Growth, Cash Flows, Risks) and Qualitative (Management, Track Record, Insights) aspects of the companies. Staying true to the nature of the fund, they diversify across market caps and asset classes. Diversification is achieved through a balanced mix of asset types, loan sizes, contract volumes, and geographies, while risk in less diversified pools is mitigated using credit enhancements. HDFC Flexi Cap maintains a strong focus on industries like Financials, Automobiles, Healthcare, Technology, etc. Fund Manager of HDFC Flexi Cap Fund, since 1 February 2026 Fund Manager of HDFC Flexi Cap Fund, since 22 June 2023 All changes are between Nov'25 and Jan'26 HDFC Flexi Cap Fund VS Parag Parikh Flexi Cap Fund Invest Now Mutual Fund execution provided by INDstocks Private Limited (Formerly known as INDmoney Private Limited) is a SEBI registered Stock Broker and mutual funds distributor. The SBI Contra Fund is an equity mutual fund from SBI Mutual Fund that invests in out-of-favour or undervalued stocks (a contrarian strategy) to achieve long-term capital appreciation. It employs a combination of top-down and bottom-up analysis to pick stocks across large-cap, mid-cap, and small-cap segments. The strategy focuses on assets that are currently unpopular, aiming to profit when market sentiment changes. The primary benchmark for the fund is the BSE 500 Total Return Index (TRI), and its secondary benchmark is the BSE SENSEX TRI. Currently, the Fund size (AUM) stands at approximately ₹49,218 Crore . Its top holdings are HDFC Bank Ltd, ICICI Bank Ltd, Axis Bank Ltd, SBI Life Insurance Co Ltd, Maruti Suzuki India Ltd As it is a elss (tax savings) mutual fund the taxation is as follows: For short term (less than a year) capital gains will be taxed at 20% For long term (more than 1 year) capital gains will be taxed at 12.5% without indexation benefit Dividends will always be taxed at slab rate. Long term gains upto Rs 1 lakh are exempt capital gains tax. To generate capital appreciation / income from a portfolio, comprising predominantly of equity & equity related instruments. There is no assurance that the investment objective of the Scheme will be realized. Minimum investment for lump sum payment is INR 500.00 and for SIP is INR 500.00. :: Securities Market Investment: A Guide to ELSS (Equity-Linked Savings Scheme) | SEBI Investor :: Home Personal Finance and Investment Securities Market Investment Content A Guide to ELSS (Equity-Linked Savings Scheme) A Guide to ELSS (Equity-Linked Savings Scheme) Equity-Linked Savings Scheme (ELSS) is a type of mutual fund designed specifically for individuals seeking tax savings under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961. These funds primarily invest in equity and equity-related instruments, offering the potential for high returns along with the added advantage of tax deductions. ELSS has gained popularity due to its dual advantage of tax efficiency and wealth creation over the long term. It is an ideal choice for those who are comfortable with market-linked investments and aim to build wealth while saving on taxes. Meaning of ELSS An ELSS is a diversified equity mutual fund where at least 80% of the corpus is invested in equity and equity-related instruments. It has a lock-in period of three years, which is the shortest among all tax-saving investment options under Section 80C. The returns, though not guaranteed, are market-linked and have the potential to outperform traditional savings instruments in the long run. How ELSS Works Investment: Investors can invest a lump sum or opt for a Systematic Investment Plan (SIP) to contribute small amounts regularly. Lock-in Period: Once invested, the amount is locked in for three years. During this period, you cannot withdraw the funds. Market Linkage: The returns depend on the performance of the equity markets as the fund manager invests in a mix of stocks across various sectors of market. Tax Deduction: Under Section 80C, an investment of up to ₹1.5 lakh in ELSS qualifies for a tax deduction in a financial year. Redemption: After the lock-in period, the investor can redeem the units or continue holding them for further growth. Benefits of ELSS Tax Savings : ELSS is a preferred option for those looking to reduce their taxable income. Wealth Creation : By investing in equities, ELSS has the potential to generate inflation-beating returns. Discipline : The lock-in period ensures disciplined investing and prevents impulsive withdrawals. Flexibility : Investors can opt for SIP or lump sum investments, depending on their financial goals and cash flow. Professional Management : The fund is managed by experienced professionals, ensuring informed investment decisions Key Features of ELSS Equity Exposure: Invests predominantly in equity and equity-related instruments. Minimum Investment: Investors can

Flexibility : Investors can opt for SIP or lump sum investments, depending on their financial goals and cash flow. Professional Management : The fund is managed by experienced professionals, ensuring informed investment decisions Key Features of ELSS Equity Exposure: Invests predominantly in equity and equity-related instruments. Minimum Investment: Investors can start with as low as Rs. 500. Risk Factor: Being equity-based, it comes with market risks, which can be mitigated through long-term investment. Growth and Dividend Options: Investors can choose between growth (capital appreciation) and dividend (periodic pay-outs) options. Tax Efficiency: Provides dual tax benefits under Section 80C and through LTCG tax exemptions.

:: Securities Market Investment: Understanding the Riskometer | SEBI Investor :: Home Personal Finance and Investment Securities Market Investment Content Understanding the Riskometer Understanding the Riskometer Investing is an essential part of personal finance, but it comes with its fair share of risks. To help investors make informed decisions, the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) introduced a tool called the Riskometer. This tool provides a clear and visual representation of the risk level associated with a particular mutual fund scheme, allowing investors to match their risk appetite with investment options. What is a Riskometer? The Riskometer is a risk-measuring tool used in the mutual fund industry to depict the risk level of a mutual fund scheme. It is mandatory for asset management companies to display the Riskometer on their schemes as per guidelines by SEBI. The Riskometer simplifies complex risk assessments, giving investors a snapshot of the potential risks involved in a fund, ranging from low to very high. How Does the Riskometer Work? The Riskometer classifies mutual fund schemes into five risk categories: Low Risk: Investments are considered safe, with minimal volatility. Ideal for conservative investors who prioritize capital protection.. Low to Moderate Risk: Slightly higher risk than low-risk funds but still relatively stable. Moderate Risk: Balanced risk level suitable for investors willing to take calculated risks. Moderately High Risk: Investments with significant exposure to market volatility. Potential for high returns but comes with higher risks. High Risk: Investment with higher risk than moderate. Potential for high return with high risk. Very High Risk: Highly volatile and risky investments, suitable for aggressive investors seeking substantial returns. Each level is color-coded to improve visual clarity for investors: Low Risk: Irish Green Low to Moderate Risk: Chartreuse Moderate Risk: Neon Yellow Moderately High Risk: Caramel High Risk: Dark Orange Very High Risk: Red The classification is based on several factors, such as: The nature of the underlying assets (equity, debt, or hybrid). Market volatility Credit risk and interest rate sensitivity. Benefits of the Riskometer Risk Assessment: The Riskometer helps investors gauge the risk level of a mutual fund scheme before investing. Decision Making: Investors can align their risk appetite with the right fund category. Transparency: It ensures that mutual funds are transparent about their risk levels, fostering trust. Simplification: The visual representation is easy to understand, making it accessible to novice investors. Enhanced Awareness: Investors become more aware of

before investing. Decision Making: Investors can align their risk appetite with the right fund category. Transparency: It ensures that mutual funds are transparent about their risk levels, fostering trust. Simplification: The visual representation is easy to understand, making it accessible to novice investors. Enhanced Awareness: Investors become more aware of the risk-return trade-off. Informed Decisions: It helps investors choose funds that match their financial goals and risk tolerance. Standardized Tool: Being a regulatory mandate, it ensures consistency across all fund houses. Prevention of Mismatched Investments: Avoids scenarios where low-risk investors end up in high-risk schemes inadvertently. The risk levels are reviewed periodically and updated based on changing market conditions Fund houses must include the Riskometer in their fact sheets and promotional materials. It applies to all mutual fund categories, including equity, debt, and hybrid funds. It is required to adhere to SEBI guidelines, ensuring uniformity.

text: Services > Addition Information > Request Statement Request Statement We offer you the convenience of having your account statement sent to your email id registered with us. Please enter your folio number in the box provided below and choose the option in which you wish to receive your account statement. Pursuant to SEBI guidelines, with effect from October 1, 2011, an allotment confirmation specifying the units allotted shall be sent by way of email and/or SMS within 5 Business Days of the initial investment transaction to the Unit holders who have registered their e-mail address and/or mobile number. Thereafter, a Consolidated Account Statement (CAS) shall be sent to the Unit holder in whose folio transactions have taken place during that month, on or before 10th of the succeeding month by mail/ email as applicable. In case of specific request received from investors, Mutual Fund will provide an account statement to the investors within 5 Business Days from the receipt of such request. Click here to view the addendum Click here for FAQ's Request statement Account statement - Click here Dividend statement - Click here Exit load statement - Click here Capital Gains Statement - Click here Consolidated account statement - Click here Consolidated realized gains statement - Click here Account statement by SMS SMS to be sent as : >CAMS H SOA to 56767, e.g.CAMS H SOA 123456 ab785z Response : Will either return an SMS confirmation that an account statement will be emailed; or in the event of error, will report the appropriate error. Account statement through IVR Call us on 1800-3010-6767 (Toll Free) / 1800-419-7676 and follow the instructions . Investors calling from abroad may call on 91-44-33462406 to connect to our Call Center. Our call center executives will be available for assistance from 9:00 am to 6:00 pm (Monday to Friday) and 9:00 am to 1:00 pm (Saturday). You can also request for an account statement across the counter by visiting any of our Investor Service Center. Click here to view ISC details.

This INDMoney mutual fund chatbot is intended only for questions about the specific mutual fund schemes it knows about (for example, NAV, exit load, lock-in period, AUM, or returns for a given scheme) and for how-to questions like downloading mutual fund or capital gains statements from official sites. Any user question that is not about these supported mutual fund schemes or related statements should be answered with: 'This question is out of scope for this INDMoney chatbot.'
